class.name: Octonion
class.brief_description: Represents rotation in 4D using an Octonion for smooth rotation interpolation.
class.description: Represents rotation in 4D using an Octonion. Octonions are an 8D number system that can be used to represent rotation in 4D, similarly to how [Quaternion]s are a 4D number system that can be used to represent rotation in 3D. Octonions only represent a valid rotation in 4D when they are normalized. The Octonion number system (r, i, j, k, l, m, n, o) is a superset of the [Quaternion] number system (r, i, j, k, or as Godot calls it, w, x, y, z), just like how [Quaternion] is a superset of complex numbers (r, i).

link.title: Multiplication table of the unit Octonions
Visualizing Quaternions with stereographic projection by 3Blue1Brown

constructor.name: Octonion
constructor.description: Constructs a default [Octonion] representing no rotation, with the real component set to 1 and all other components set to 0.

constructor.name: Octonion
constructor.description: Constructs a [Octonion] from an existing [Quaternion]. As such, the only rotation will be in 3D.

constructor.name: Octonion
constructor.description: Constructs an [Octonion] from the given real and imaginary components. Unlike [Quaternion], the real component comes first. The order is (r, i, j, k, l, m, n, o).

method.name: is_equal_approx
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if this [Octonion] and [code]octonion[/code] are approximately equal, by running [method @GlobalScope.is_equal_approx] on each component.

members.member: Imaginary [code]i[/code] axis component of the [Octonion]. By index, this is 1. This is equivalent to the X component of [Quaternion].
Imaginary [code]j[/code] axis component of the [Octonion]. By index, this is 2. This is equivalent to the Y component of [Quaternion].
Imaginary [code]k[/code] axis component of the [Octonion]. By index, this is 3. This is equivalent to the Z component of [Quaternion].
Imaginary [code]l[/code] axis component of the [Octonion]. By index, this is 4.
Imaginary [code]m[/code] axis component of the [Octonion]. By index, this is 5.
Imaginary [code]n[/code] axis component of the [Octonion]. By index, this is 6.
Imaginary [code]o[/code] axis component of the [Octonion]. By index, this is 7.
The real component of the [Octonion]. By index, this is 0. This is equivalent to the W component of [Quaternion].

constants.constant: The identity [Octonion], representing no rotation. The real part is set to 1 and all imaginary parts are set to 0.

operator.name: operator !=
operator.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the [Octonion]s are not equal. [b]Note:[/b] Due to floating-point precision errors, consider using [method is_equal_approx] instead, which is more reliable.

operator.name: operator *
operator.description: Multiplies two [Octonion]s together. [b]Note:[/b] Octonion multiplication is neither commutative nor associative. The order and grouping of multiplication matters.

operator.name: operator ==
operator.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the [Octonion]s are exactly equal. [b]Note:[/b] Due to floating-point precision errors, consider using [method is_equal_approx] instead, which is more reliable.

operator.name: operator []
operator.description: Access the [Octonion] components by index, in the order (r, i, j, k, l, m, n, o).

operator.name: operator unary-
operator.description: Returns the negative value of the [Octonion]. This is the same as writing [code]Octonion(-o.r, -o.i, -o.j, -o.k, -o.l, -o.m, -o.n, -o.o)[/code].
